title: leptosiphon nuttallii subsp. howellii
intro: Leptosiphon nuttallii subsp. howellii, also known as Howell's linanthus, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the polemoniaceae family. It is a herbaceous perennial that grows to be about 10-30 cm tall, with small white or pinkish flowers and hairy leaves. Leptosiphon nuttallii subsp. howellii has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
